subject: Physics
title: Why Sunsets Are Red
description: Learn Why Sunsets Are Red in one minute.

hook.text: At sunset, sunlight passes through more atmosphere, and the sky blazes orange and red.

buildup.text: When the Sun is low, its light cuts through a much thicker layer of air than at noon.

discovery.text: All the blue light scatters away before reaching you. Only longer wavelengths—red and orange—survive.

twist.text: Volcanic eruptions and wildfires add particles that make sunsets even more vivid. Pollution paints the sky.

climax.text: Mars has blue sunsets because its dust scatters red and lets blue through—the opposite of Earth.

punchline.text: Long path, lost blue, leftover red. Physics paints the sunset.

quiz.question: Why are sunsets red and orange?
options: Blue light scatters away over the longer atmospheric path
The Sun changes color as it cools in the evening
Clouds absorb blue light at sunset
